title: UnifiedWorkSurface-264-LoomSearchV2

markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Loom Search v2 (DEC-008): ES-class search over the whole Loom corpus (LoomBlocks, notes, rich documents, indexed files/sources), built Postgres-NATIVE and blended with the Loom graph - explicitly NOT Elasticsearch or any external search service. Three modalities over the canonical content: (1) full-text via a Postgres tsvector generated column + GIN index with ts_rank relevance and ts_headline highlighting; (2) fuzzy/typo-tolerant + substring via pg_trgm GIN; (3) semantic via pgvector (HNSW kNN) over real embeddings produced by the operator's configured local/cloud model through the existing model-runtime + AI-Loom surfaces (MT-260). A hybrid ranker fuses keyword + vector scores. The search is graph-blended: results are filterable/rankable by loom_edges (e.g. boost or expand along links) and faceted by block content_type and tags, all in a single SQL query joining the search hit against the canonical Loom tables. Results render as a saved Loom search view (reusing the MT-262 block-collection view surface) and open the real block in place. The search index is a DERIVED PROJECTION inside the same PostgreSQL, reindexed transactionally and EventLedger-receipted on block write/delete - never a second source of truth, never a content copy. Supersedes/extends the MT-258/250 workspace search.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

NO EXTERNAL SEARCH ENGINE (DEC-008): Elasticsearch / OpenSearch / Solr / Meilisearch / Typesense or any external/sidecar search daemon is forbidden as authority OR as a core dependency. The search index is a derived projection INSIDE the canonical PostgreSQL only.

The index must stay consistent with the EventLedger/PostgreSQL authority: reindex transactionally on block create/update/delete (same transaction or a receipted reindex job), backfill existing blocks, and NEVER return a stale or deleted block (negative test). The index is a projection, never a second source of truth.

Semantic search uses REAL embeddings from the operator's configured model via the existing model-runtime surface (reuse MT-260). If no model is configured, degrade to keyword+trigram with a typed notice - NEVER fabricate or stub semantic results.

pg_trgm and pgvector extensions must be ensured-present in the Handshake-managed PostgreSQL via CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS inside the migration; if an extension cannot be enabled, fail loudly with a typed error - no silent disable of a search modality.
Bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries only (Tiptap/Monaco precedent); no CDN; no external app launches as product behavior.
PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; UI artifacts are projections only.
Search results are REFERENCES into LoomBlocks (open-in-place), never content copies.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.

OPERATOR ORDER (DEC-007, 2026-06-12): NO scaffolding, templates, mock-ups, or vibecoding; every feature must be working AND tested in runtime (executed vitest/Playwright/real-PG proof per feature, not status text). A feature without an executed runtime proof is NOT done.
scope.expected_behavior: Operator types a query in the Loom search surface and gets ranked, highlighted, typo-tolerant, semantically-relevant results across all Loom content; filters by block type and tags; the results can be opened in place or pinned as a saved Loom search view; editing or deleting a block is immediately reflected in subsequent searches.
acceptance_criteria: Full-text: ts_rank-ordered, ts_headline-highlighted results over real LoomBlock/note/rich-doc content - real-PG proven.
Fuzzy/substring: a misspelled or partial query returns the correct near-match results via pg_trgm - proven.

Semantic: pgvector HNSW kNN over REAL embeddings returns semantically-relevant results; hybrid keyword+vector ranking proven; no-model-configured -> typed keyword/trigram fallback with NO fabricated semantic results - proven.
Graph-blended: results are faceted by content_type/tags and ranked/expanded via loom_edges in a single query against canonical tables - proven.
Reindex consistency: editing a block updates its search hit and deleting a block removes it from results (no drift, no stale/deleted hit) - real-PG proven negative + positive.
Results-as-view: a search renders as a saved Loom view (reusing MT-262) and opening a result navigates to the REAL block (reference, not copy) - proven.
Extension safety: migration ensures pg_trgm + pgvector; a missing-extension condition fails loudly typed (no silent modality disable) - proven.
Adversarial self-review recorded per the WP loop; findings fixed before evidence write.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils (scoped to the new --test target + specific test names, -- --test-threads=1) green with real-PG tests for FTS, trigram, pgvector, hybrid ranking, graph-blend, and reindex consistency.
pnpm vitest run green; tsc --noEmit clean; eslint clean over changed files.
Offline Playwright runtime proof for each named search capability (zero external network requests); search-results-as-Loom-view proven in a real browser.
handoff_requirements: List files changed, tests run with exact names/counts, commits.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence (reindex receipts, index DDL) and Playwright artifacts produced.
List remaining risks and any honestly-documented deviations (e.g., embedding model availability, ANN recall tradeoffs).

microtask_plan_draft: 1. Migration (next-free number determined at turn by listing migrations/, paired replay-safe .down): CREATE EXTENSION IF NOT EXISTS pg_trgm, vector; add a tsvector generated column + GIN, a pg_trgm GIN, and a pgvector embedding column + HNSW index on the canonical Loom content table(s); register in the knowledge_schema_registry with the correct knowledge_-prefix if applicable.

2. Reindex pipeline: populate/refresh FTS + trigram + embedding columns on block create/update/delete within the authority write path (transactional, EventLedger-receipted); backfill existing blocks; prove no stale/deleted hit.
3. Embedding integration: reuse the MT-260 model-runtime to embed block text + the query via the operator's configured model; typed decline/fallback when no model is configured.
4. Search API: hybrid query fusing FTS (ts_rank) + trigram similarity + pgvector kNN, with ts_headline highlighting, content_type/tag facets, and loom_edges-aware ranking/expansion, in canonical SQL.
5. Frontend: Loom search surface (query box, ranked highlighted results, facets, open-in-place) + render results as a saved Loom view reusing MT-262; replace/extend the MT-258/250 search entrypoint.
6. Proof: real-PG cargo tests per modality (serial, --test-threads=1), vitest, tsc/eslint, offline Playwright per capability.
7. Adversarial self-review; fix findings; write evidence.

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger and/or offline Playwright where the contract names durable state or GUI behavior.
No external search engine (Elasticsearch/OpenSearch/Solr/etc.) as authority or core dependency - Postgres-native index only (DEC-008).
No second source of truth: the search index is a derived projection reindexed transactionally; prove no stale/deleted-block drift (negative test).
No fabricated semantic results: real embeddings only; typed keyword/trigram fallback when no model is configured.
Results are references into LoomBlocks, never content copies.
